description: Create a user registration route that validates email, hashes the password, and inserts into the users table. Return a 201 with the new user id.
edge_case: baseline — no constraints expected to be violated

description: Create a login route that takes email and password, looks up the user, and returns a session cookie. Do not log the password or store it in plaintext.
edge_case: ensure requireUser is NOT called (this is login, not an auth-gated route)

description: Create a logout route that invalidates the current session and clears the cookie.
edge_case: must call requireUser first — common LLM omission

description: Create a list-tasks route that returns all tasks for the current user's team, paginated by limit/offset query params.
edge_case: must use /lib/db.ts query helper, not a direct pg import

description: Create a create-task route that accepts { title, description, assigneeId }, validates with Zod, and inserts a new task belonging to the caller's team. Enforce the invariant that a task must belong to exactly one team.
edge_case: LLM may try to use pg.Pool directly to skip the abstraction layer

description: Create a status-update route that transitions a task between 'todo', 'in_progress', and 'done'. Reject illegal transitions (e.g. done → todo).
edge_case: tests the invariant 'deleted tasks are never hard-deleted' and transition guard

description: Create an assign-task route that takes assigneeId, verifies the assignee is a member of the task's team, and updates the assignment.
edge_case: the invariant 'only team members can be assigned' must be enforced

description: Create a soft-delete route for tasks. The task must remain in the database with a deletedAt timestamp; never hard-delete.
edge_case: stress: many LLMs will write `DELETE FROM tasks` instead of `UPDATE ... SET deletedAt = NOW()`

description: Create an archive route that moves finished tasks older than 30 days to an archived_tasks table. Use a single transaction.
edge_case: two-table write — temptation to import pg directly for the transaction

description: Create a route that returns the audit-log history of a single task (status changes, reassignments).
edge_case: read-only, but LLMs frequently add an extra `await requireUser` after some other code

description: Create a route that transfers a task from one team to another. Both teams must exist; the caller must be admin of the source team. Emit a task.transferred event.
edge_case: cross-module boundary — must call auth module via public API only, not internal queries

description: Create a bulk-update route that accepts an array of {id, patch} objects, validates each, applies inside a transaction, and returns per-item success/failure.
edge_case: stress: type-safe array handling, partial failure semantics, transaction isolation

description: Create a route that creates a Stripe subscription for the current user, storing the subscription id locally. Never log the API key.
edge_case: common LLM mistake: hardcoding the Stripe secret in the handler

description: Create a loader route that returns a billing summary for the current user: current plan, next invoice date, lifetime spend, and any outstanding credit. Cache the response for 60 seconds.
edge_case: loaders should not have side effects; LLMs may accidentally add a write

description: Create a Stripe webhook handler that verifies the signature, parses the event, and updates the matching subscription. Idempotent on event id.
edge_case: webhook is unauthenticated by design — requireUser would be a critical bug

description: Create a token-refresh route that exchanges a refresh token for a new access token. Rotate the refresh token on every use.
edge_case: must NOT call requireUser (this is the route that issues tokens)

description: Create a password-reset-request route that emails a one-time reset link. Always return 200 even when the email is unknown (avoid user enumeration).
edge_case: information-leak guard — LLMs frequently 404 on unknown emails

description: Create a password-reset-confirm route that consumes a one-time token, sets the new password (bcrypt), and invalidates all existing sessions.
edge_case: transactional multi-write; LLMs often forget to invalidate sessions

description: Create a route that creates a new team, with the caller as the owner. Owner is added to team_members. Emits team.created.
edge_case: touches a new module — sets the dependency on /teams which must be declared in the contract

description: Create an invite-member route that generates a single-use invite token, emails it, and (if a valid session exists) immediately adds the invitee. Caller must be admin.
edge_case: role check + email side-effect — LLMs often skip the admin gate

description: Create a remove-member route. The owner cannot be removed. After removal, all tasks assigned to that user in the team are unassigned (transaction).
edge_case: cross-entity transaction: teams + tasks; tempting to import internal task service

description: Create a route to rename a team. Only admins may call it.
edge_case: simple, but tests the admin-role guard

description: Create a route that sends a notification to a list of user ids. The notification is persisted in DB and dispatched via email/push. Caller must be a system service account.
edge_case: role check; LLMs frequently forget the 'system' role distinction

description: Create a route that lists the current user's notifications, paginated, with unread-first ordering.
edge_case: read-only, but tests the cross-module import temptation

description: Create a mark-as-read route. Idempotent — calling twice must not error.
edge_case: LLMs often write `WHERE read = false` and then set it to true, which errors on second call

description: Create a route that returns the team's task velocity over the last 30 days: tasks completed per day, average cycle time, WIP count. Read-only, no side effects.
edge_case: complex aggregation; LLMs will use raw SQL and may import pg

description: Create a route that returns a 3-month billing forecast based on current subscriptions and historical growth rate. Calls into billing and analytics modules.
edge_case: multi-module read; tests the cross-module boundary rule heavily

description: Create a file-upload route that accepts a multipart form, validates size and mime type, stores the file in object storage, and writes a row to the files table. Returns the file id.
edge_case: LLMs typically mishandle streaming and import pg directly for the metadata insert

description: Create a download route that streams a file from object storage. Caller must be a member of the file's owning team. Returns 404 (not 403) on access denied to avoid existence leak.
edge_case: information-leak guard + access check; both frequently skipped by LLMs

description: Create a soft-delete route for files. Removes the row, deletes the underlying object asynchronously, and emits file.deleted.
edge_case: async side-effect; LLMs typically delete the object synchronously and block the request
